question.text: A trader's collateral utilization reaches 90% of the total deposited margin. What automatic action does the exchange system trigger?

answer.text: All unexecuted pending orders are cancelled automatically
The trading terminal is permanently disabled
The trader's existing positions are forcibly liquidated
Additional margin of 50% is automatically collected

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When collateral utilization hits 90%, the exchange's risk management system automatically cancels all pending (unexecuted) orders to prevent further exposure. This is a pre-emptive risk control measure. **The Trap:** The terminal is not permanently disabled (it can be reactivated with additional margin). Positions are not immediately liquidated (that happens at higher utilization). Additional margin is not auto-collected. **Cross-Exam Context:** The 90% collateral trigger is specific to IRD/currency segments. Equity derivatives may have different thresholds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Traders should monitor their margin utilization and top up collateral before reaching 90% to avoid disruption of their order strategy.
